text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Lungs are clear bilaterally without evidence of infiltrates or effusions. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. No bony abnormalities noted.
IMPRESSION: Normal chest radiograph.
impression: Normal chest radiograph.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Mild bilateral apical pleural thickening noted. No acute process seen in lung fields. Heart size is within normal limits.
IMPRESSION: Mild pleural thickening, likely chronic.
impression: Mild pleural thickening, likely chronic.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Right lower lobe consolidation with air bronchograms. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. Cardiac silhouette is normal.
IMPRESSION: Right lower lobe pneumonia.
impression: Right lower lobe pneumonia.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Bilateral pulmonary nodules measuring up to 1.5 cm, with no associated lymphadenopathy. Heart and mediastinum appear normal.
IMPRESSION: Bilateral pulmonary nodules, further evaluation recommended.
impression: Bilateral pulmonary nodules, further evaluation recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Clear lung fields bilaterally. No signs of cardiomegaly or effusion. Diaphragms are well-defined.
IMPRESSION: Normal chest radiograph.
impression: Normal chest radiograph.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Left-sided pleural effusion evident with blunting of the left costophrenic angle. Heart size is normal. No acute lung pathology seen.
IMPRESSION: Left pleural effusion.
impression: Left pleural effusion.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Lung fields demonstrate patchy infiltrates in the left upper lobe. No pleural effusion or significant lymphadenopathy noted. Heart is normal in size.
IMPRESSION: Suspected left upper lobe pneumonia.
impression: Suspected left upper lobe pneumonia.

text:
TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest radiograph.
FINDINGS: Bilateral hyperinflated lungs with flattened diaphragms. No acute infiltrates or effusions. Heart size is within normal limits.
IMPRESSION: Findings consistent with emphysema.
impression: Findings consistent with emphysema.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest without contrast
FINDINGS: Mild bilateral ground-glass opacities in the lower lobes. No pleural effusion or lymphadenopathy noted.
IMPRESSION: Findings suggestive of early viral pneumonia.
impression: Findings suggestive of early viral pneumonia.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest with contrast
FINDINGS: Well-defined 2.5 cm mass in the right upper lobe with surrounding ground-glass opacity. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy.
IMPRESSION: Suspicious for primary lung malignancy; further evaluation recommended.
impression: Suspicious for primary lung malignancy; further evaluation recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest without contrast
FINDINGS: Normal lung parenchyma with no evidence of consolidation or nodules. Heart size and mediastinal structures are unremarkable.
IMPRESSION: Normal chest CT.
impression: Normal chest CT.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest with contrast
FINDINGS: Subcentimeter nodules in the left lower lobe, largest measuring 0.8 cm. No significant lymphadenopathy or pleural effusions.
IMPRESSION: Stable pulmonary nodules; follow-up recommended.
impression: Stable pulmonary nodules; follow-up recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest without contrast
FINDINGS: Bilateral pleural effusions with associated atelectasis in the lower lobes. No suspicious masses identified.
IMPRESSION: Pleural effusions possibly secondary to congestive heart failure.
impression: Pleural effusions possibly secondary to congestive heart failure.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest with contrast
FINDINGS: Large 4 cm cavitary lesion in the right lower lobe. No evidence of vascular invasion or significant lymphadenopathy.
IMPRESSION: Cavitary lesion raises concern for infectious process; further evaluation with follow-up imaging suggested.
impression: Cavitary lesion raises concern for infectious process; further evaluation with follow-up imaging suggested.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest without contrast
FINDINGS: Mild interstitial thickening noted throughout the lungs. No acute infiltrates or nodules present.
IMPRESSION: Findings consistent with early interstitial lung disease.
impression: Findings consistent with early interstitial lung disease.

text:
TECHNIQUE: CT chest with contrast
FINDINGS: Multiple bilateral pulmonary emboli noted in the segmental arteries. Right heart strain is observed.
IMPRESSION: Acute pulmonary embolism; urgent clinical correlation required.
impression: Acute pulmonary embolism; urgent clinical correlation required.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain without contrast
FINDINGS: The brain shows normal morphology with no evidence of mass effect. The ventricles are symmetrical and unremarkable. No abnormal signal intensities are noted in the white or gray matter.
IMPRESSION: Normal MRI of the brain.
impression: Normal MRI of the brain.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain with and without contrast
FINDINGS: There is a 2.5 cm enhancing lesion in the right frontal lobe consistent with a low-grade glioma. Surrounding edema is present. The ventricles are mildly compressed.
IMPRESSION: Suspected low-grade glioma in the right frontal lobe.
impression: Suspected low-grade glioma in the right frontal lobe.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain without contrast
FINDINGS: The brain parenchyma is unremarkable. No focal lesions or areas of abnormal signal are identified. The midline structures are in normal alignment.
IMPRESSION: Normal MRI findings.
impression: Normal MRI findings.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain with contrast
FINDINGS: There is a 1.8 cm enhancing lesion in the left temporal lobe with surrounding vasogenic edema. No midline shift is observed. The basal ganglia and thalami are normal.
IMPRESSION: Possible metastatic lesion in the left temporal lobe.
impression: Possible metastatic lesion in the left temporal lobe.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain without contrast
FINDINGS: Mild prominence of the lateral and third ventricles with no significant parenchymal atrophy. No acute ischemic changes are seen.
IMPRESSION: Mild age-related changes; no acute intracranial pathology.
impression: Mild age-related changes; no acute intracranial pathology.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain with and without contrast
FINDINGS: A 3 cm cystic lesion is noted in the right parietal lobe with a thin rim of enhancement, suggesting a low-grade tumor versus a cystic metastasis. No significant mass effect.
IMPRESSION: Cystic lesion in the right parietal lobe; further evaluation recommended.
impression: Cystic lesion in the right parietal lobe; further evaluation recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain without contrast
FINDINGS: The cerebral cortex shows normal thickness. No lesions or abnormal signal intensities are evident. The cerebellum and brainstem appear normal.
IMPRESSION: Normal MRI of the brain.
impression: Normal MRI of the brain.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain with contrast
FINDINGS: There is an 8 mm enhancing nodule in the left occipital lobe. No associated edema or mass effect is noted. Other structures are unremarkable.
IMPRESSION: Enhancing nodule in the left occipital lobe; differential includes a primary brain tumor or metastasis.
impression: Enhancing nodule in the left occipital lobe; differential includes a primary brain tumor or metastasis.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound performed using a curvilinear transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is normal in size with homogeneous echogenicity. The gallbladder is visualized, containing no stones. The pancreas appears unremarkable. The kidneys are normal in size with no evidence of hydronephrosis. No free fluid is noted in the abdominal cavity.
IMPRESSION: Normal abdominal ultrasound.
impression: Normal abdominal ultrasound.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound performed with a high-frequency transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver shows mild hepatomegaly with increased echogenicity suggestive of steatosis. The gallbladder is mildly distended but without stones. Mild pericholecystic fluid is noted. Both kidneys appear normal. No masses or lymphadenopathy is identified.
IMPRESSION: Mild hepatic steatosis; otherwise unremarkable abdominal ultrasound.
impression: Mild hepatic steatosis; otherwise unremarkable abdominal ultrasound.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound conducted with a low-frequency transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is normal in echogenicity. The gallbladder contains multiple small stones. The pancreas is normal. The right kidney demonstrates mild hydronephrosis. The left kidney is unremarkable. No abdominal free fluid is observed.
IMPRESSION: Cholelithiasis with mild hydronephrosis of the right kidney.
impression: Cholelithiasis with mild hydronephrosis of the right kidney.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound performed using an abdominal curvilinear transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is normal in size and echogenicity. The gallbladder is normal with no stones. The pancreas shows slight enlargement with heterogeneous echogenicity. The kidneys are normal. There is a small amount of free fluid in the pelvis.
IMPRESSION: Possible pancreatitis with pelvic free fluid; further evaluation recommended.
impression: Possible pancreatitis with pelvic free fluid; further evaluation recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound performed with a curvilinear transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is within normal limits. The gallbladder is normal. The pancreas shows normal echogenicity and contour. Kidneys are normal in size with no evidence of obstruction. No free fluid or masses are identified.
IMPRESSION: Normal findings on abdominal ultrasound.
impression: Normal findings on abdominal ultrasound.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound conducted using a high-frequency transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver shows a focal hypoechoic lesion measuring 2 cm in the right lobe. The gallbladder is normal. The pancreas is unremarkable. The kidneys are normal without hydronephrosis. No significant free fluid is present.
IMPRESSION: Focal hepatic lesion; further evaluation recommended.
impression: Focal hepatic lesion; further evaluation recommended.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound performed using a curvilinear transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is normal in size but echogenicity suggests mild steatosis. The gallbladder is normal. The pancreas appears normal. The right kidney shows signs of mild hydronephrosis while the left kidney is unremarkable. No free fluid is noted.
IMPRESSION: Mild hepatic steatosis and right hydronephrosis.
impression: Mild hepatic steatosis and right hydronephrosis.

text:
TECHNIQUE: Abdominal ultrasound conducted with a low-frequency transducer.
FINDINGS: The liver is enlarged with a heterogeneous echotexture. The gallbladder is normal. The pancreas appears normal. There is moderate fluid accumulation in the abdominal cavity. Kidneys are normal.
IMPRESSION: Hepatomegaly with ascites; further evaluation required.
impression: Hepatomegaly with ascites; further evaluation required.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the right wrist.
FINDINGS: No acute fractures or dislocations. The bones appear intact with normal alignment. Soft tissues are unremarkable.
IMPRESSION: Normal right wrist radiographs.
impression: Normal right wrist radiographs.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the left ankle.
FINDINGS: There is a small avulsion fracture of the lateral malleolus. Mild soft tissue swelling noted around the ankle joint.
IMPRESSION: Acute lateral malleolus avulsion fracture.
impression: Acute lateral malleolus avulsion fracture.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the right knee.
FINDINGS: The joint space is well maintained. No effusion is seen. Bones are intact with no signs of fracture.
IMPRESSION: Normal right knee radiographs.
impression: Normal right knee radiographs.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP, lateral, and oblique views of the left hand.
FINDINGS: There is a comminuted fracture of the proximal phalanx of the index finger. Soft tissue swelling and hematoma are observed.
IMPRESSION: Fracture of the proximal phalanx of the left index finger.
impression: Fracture of the proximal phalanx of the left index finger.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the right foot.
FINDINGS: No fractures identified. The bones are normally aligned. Some mild degenerative changes are noted at the first metatarsophalangeal joint.
IMPRESSION: Normal right foot radiographs with mild degenerative changes.
impression: Normal right foot radiographs with mild degenerative changes.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the left shoulder.
FINDINGS: There is a slight superior migration of the humeral head. No acute fracture is appreciated. Mild joint effusion is noted.
IMPRESSION: Possible rotator cuff pathology; recommend clinical correlation.
impression: Possible rotator cuff pathology; recommend clinical correlation.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the right elbow.
FINDINGS: The radiographs demonstrate a small, displaced fracture of the radial head. Joint effusion is present.
IMPRESSION: Displaced fracture of the radial head in the right elbow.
impression: Displaced fracture of the radial head in the right elbow.

text:
TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral views of the left hip.
FINDINGS: No fractures or dislocations. The joint space is well preserved. Soft tissues appear normal.
IMPRESSION: Normal left hip radiographs.
impression: Normal left hip radiographs.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Mild degenerative disc changes at L4-L5 with a small central disc bulge. No significant spinal canal stenosis. Facet joints appear unremarkable.
IMPRESSION: Mild degenerative changes at L4-L5; no acute abnormalities.
impression: Mild degenerative changes at L4-L5; no acute abnormalities.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Normal alignment of the lumbar spine. Intervertebral discs are well hydrated. No evidence of disc herniation or spinal stenosis. The conus medullaris is normal.
IMPRESSION: Normal MRI of the lumbar spine.
impression: Normal MRI of the lumbar spine.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Moderate disc degeneration at L5-S1 with a broad-based posterior disc bulge causing mild left lateral recess narrowing. No significant nerve root compression.
IMPRESSION: Moderate degenerative changes at L5-S1; mild lateral recess narrowing.
impression: Moderate degenerative changes at L5-S1; mild lateral recess narrowing.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Severe disc bulge at L3-L4 with associated foraminal narrowing bilaterally. Mild spinal canal stenosis noted. No acute fracture or significant edema.
IMPRESSION: Severe disc bulge at L3-L4 with foraminal narrowing; suggest clinical correlation.
impression: Severe disc bulge at L3-L4 with foraminal narrowing; suggest clinical correlation.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Slight lordosis with well-maintained disc spaces. No evidence of herniation or stenosis. The paravertebral soft tissues are unremarkable.
IMPRESSION: Unremarkable MRI of the lumbar spine.
impression: Unremarkable MRI of the lumbar spine.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Small right paracentral disc herniation at L4-L5 with mild right-sided nerve root impingement. No significant spinal canal stenosis.
IMPRESSION: Right paracentral disc herniation at L4-L5; mild nerve root impingement.
impression: Right paracentral disc herniation at L4-L5; mild nerve root impingement.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: Mild scoliosis with generalized disc desiccation. No herniation or stenosis evident. The spinal cord and nerve roots appear intact.
IMPRESSION: Mild scoliosis with degenerative changes; no acute findings.
impression: Mild scoliosis with degenerative changes; no acute findings.

text:
TECHNIQUE: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
FINDINGS: L3-L4 disc herniation with significant central canal stenosis and mild compression of the thecal sac. No nerve root involvement seen.
IMPRESSION: L3-L4 disc herniation with central canal stenosis; recommend orthopedic evaluation.
impression: L3-L4 disc herniation with central canal stenosis; recommend orthopedic evaluation.
